summary: Added parsing of DTK-style `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to the Rust `tools/config-dtk` tool while retaining map-file parsing/output generation. The PR introduced a new `dtk_config` parser module using `nom`/`nom-supreme`, renamed the existing CodeWarrior map parsing context to `Parser`, and adjusted `main.rs` to read both the generated map and the checked-in config symbols file. This progresses issue #1082. There was no recorded review feedback in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added `tools/config-dtk/src/dtk_config.rs`, defining a `Symbol<'a>` struct for parsed symbols with fields for name, address, section, optional size, align, type, data, scope, and hidden status.
Implemented `dtk_config::Parser::parse_symbols`, which parses `symbols.txt` lines in parallel with Rayon and stores symbols in a `BTreeMap<u32, Symbol<'a>>` keyed by address.
Implemented a `nom` parser for lines of the form `name = section:0xADDR; // attr:value ...`, including attributes such as `size`, `align`, `type`, `data`, `scope`, and `hidden`.
Updated `tools/config-dtk/src/main.rs` to read `build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map`, output to `build/GALE01/symbols.txt`, and additionally read `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` for parsing.
Refactored path handling in `main.rs` through a `root_path` helper that joins paths against `melee_utils::ROOT` and canonicalizes them.
Renamed the existing `cw_map::Context<'a>` type to `cw_map::Parser<'a>` to avoid a generic-sounding parser state name and to align with the new `dtk_config::Parser` module.
Simplified `cw_map::Parser::new` from returning `Result<Self>` to returning `Self`, because construction no longer had fallible work.
Replaced memory-mapped file reading in `config-dtk` with `fs::read_to_string` for the map and symbols input files.

Updated `tools/config-dtk/Cargo.toml`: added `nom` and `nom-supreme`, bumped `env_logger` to `0.11.0`, and removed unused dependencies including `globset`, `lazy_static`, `memmap2`, and `string-interner` from this tool.
Updated `Cargo.lock` for dependency changes, including a newer `cwparse` git revision and new transitive dependencies for `nom-supreme` and `env_logger 0.11.0`.
smart_moves: Separated parsing responsibilities into modules: `cw_map` now parses the CodeWarrior map, while the new `dtk_config` module parses DTK `symbols.txt` content.
Used `nom-supreme::error::ErrorTree` and converted parser failures through `anyhow` with context, giving more structured parse errors than ad hoc string splitting would provide.
Preserved lifetimes by parsing symbol names and attributes as `&'a str` slices instead of eagerly allocating strings.
Stored parsed config symbols in a `BTreeMap` keyed by address, which provides deterministic address-ordered traversal useful for config generation and comparisons.
Parsed lines with Rayon `par_lines`, mirroring the existing tool’s parallel parsing style and keeping the approach scalable for large symbol files.
Used Rust raw identifier syntax `r#type` for the `type` attribute, retaining the file format’s terminology without conflicting with the Rust keyword.
Made the existing map parser type name less ambiguous by changing `Context` to `Parser`, especially important after adding a second parser module.
decomp_lessons: When adding tooling for decomp config files, isolate file-format parsers by source format rather than mixing generated map parsing and checked-in config parsing in one context object.
For symbol files, parse and retain structured attributes instead of treating comment text as opaque; later tooling can then compare size, alignment, scope, hidden status, and section data directly.
Prefer deterministic containers such as `BTreeMap` when output order or address-order analysis matters.
A small helper for repository-root-relative paths reduces duplicated `melee_utils::ROOT.join(...).canonicalize()` logic and makes tool entry points easier to audit.
Parser naming matters in multi-format tools: avoid generic names like `Context` once the tool has more than one parsing state.
Use parser combinators for strict config syntax when the expected line format is well-defined; this catches malformed `symbols.txt` entries early.
Removing stale dependencies from tool-specific `Cargo.toml` keeps Rust decomp tooling easier to build and maintain.
naming_conventions: `cw_map::Context` was renamed to `cw_map::Parser`, matching its role and aligning with the new `dtk_config::Parser`.
The new module name `dtk_config` identifies parsing of DTK config files rather than generated CodeWarrior map output.
The parsed symbol type is named `Symbol<'a>` and its attribute enum is named `SymbolAttribute<'a>`.
The parser method is named `parse_symbols`, explicitly tying it to `symbols.txt` rather than a generic config parse.
Symbol fields mirror DTK attribute names where possible: `size`, `align`, `data`, `scope`, `hidden`; Rust keyword conflict is handled as `r#type`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching or C decompilation changes were present in this PR.
The tooling change supports future matching/decomp work by parsing `symbols.txt` into structured symbol records, which can help compare generated map symbols against DTK config symbol metadata.
The existing generated-symbol size backfill remains: when a table symbol has size zero, the tool derives size from the previous address during reverse address traversal.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that consume `dtk_config::Parser::parse_symbols` beyond printing parsed symbols to stdout.
Check issue #1082 for the intended end state of `config-dtk` and whether this parser was part of a larger config migration.
Search for later changes to `tools/config-dtk/src/dtk_config.rs` that add tests, duplicate-address handling, or more symbol attributes.
Look for later PRs replacing the temporary stdout dump of parsed config symbols with actual comparison or generation logic.
Audit whether `symbols.txt` lines with missing comments, reordered attributes, dotted sections, or C++ names outside `cpp_name` syntax are accepted or intentionally rejected.
